architecture.summary: Bridge-STG is an end-to-end framework for spatio-temporal video grounding that decouples temporal and spatial localization while maintaining semantic coherence. A Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) performs temporal reasoning to produce temporal reasoning context. This is distilled via a Spatio-Temporal Semantic Bridging (STSB) mechanism with Explicit Temporal Alignment (ETA) into enriched bridging queries. These queries serve as a semantic interface to a purpose-built spatial decoder. The spatial decoder uses Query-Guided Spatial Localization (QGSL) with multi-layer interactive queries and positive/negative frame sampling to perform spatial localization while reducing visual token redundancy.
trained_components: Spatio-Temporal Semantic Bridging (STSB) mechanism with Explicit Temporal Alignment (ETA)

biblio.title: Bridging Time and Space: Decoupled Spatio-Temporal Alignment for Video Grounding

claims.statement: Bridge-STG improves average m_vIoU from 26.4 to 34.3 on VidSTG
Bridge-STG achieves state-of-the-art performance among MLLM-based methods across multiple benchmarks
Bridge-STG demonstrates strong cross-task transfer across various fine-grained video understanding tasks under a unified multi-task training regime
Spatio-Temporal Semantic Bridging (STSB) mechanism with Explicit Temporal Alignment (ETA) distills the MLLM's temporal reasoning context into enriched bridging queries as a robust semantic interface

Query-Guided Spatial Localization (QGSL) module leverages bridging queries to drive a purpose-built spatial decoder with multi-layer interactive queries and positive/negative frame sampling to eliminate dual-domain visual token redundancy
